criteria_checked.note: Compatibility matrix independently re-derived from repo bytes. Mock carve-out: mock-001 task declares provider_target.type='mock'/provider='mock-local' (.harness/workers/tasks/mock-001.json:19-20) while the glm adapter unconditionally stamps api_key/glm-api (glm-api-adapter.py:199-200), so v0.2.1-verify.sh:18 and all ~12 v0.2.1.1 accept() rows (accept() defaults to mock-001, dmc-v0.2.1.1-verify.sh:19) require exactly the type=='mock' skip; the committed mock-001 result itself matches (mock/mock-local) so v0.2-verify:36 survives with or without it. Empty-provider carve-out: dmc-v0.2.3-verify.sh:26 builds api_key/'' and :55-56 expects ACCEPT — glm stamps provider_type=api_key so type-equality-only is exactly right. Task floor verified against every ACCEPT-row task: all carry non-empty task_id, one-entry allowed_files, non-empty provider_target.type (v0.2.3:17-26 mktask, v0.2.2:19, v0.2.4:29-32, v0.3.1:35-38, v0.3.3:34-36); nopt.json/mock.json die at the router (v0.2.3:51-52 verified), sectask dies at context-guard (v0.2.2:105-107; v0.2.4:118-121; v0.3.1:93-95; v0.3.3:127-130 all verified). Result floor verified: both adapters' map_to_result emit every required key with defaults on ALL paths including fallbacks (glm-api-adapter.py:184-204, oauth-cli-adapter.py:224-247), and task_id is adapter-stamped from the task, so the unconditional task_id cross-check and key-presence floor cannot flip an adapter-generated ACCEPT row. Caller sweep re-run repo-wide: only the eight bin/lib verify scripts invoke the validator inside the aggregate; dmc-glm-smoke validates a runtime-only task under gitignored .harness/workers/providers/ (git ls-files empty), live-only, outside every suite. Token-shape sweep over all success/fallback fixtures (incl. the six imported OAuth classes, case-insensitive): only the three REJECT-row fixtures (cli-response-token-leak, cli-response-stderr-token-leak, import-secret) carry token material — no ACCEPT row is flipped by the broadened scan. B1 closed.

manual-import-adapter.py:84-85 imports BOTH DISALLOWED and diff_paths from worker-result-check.py; Rev 2 pins diff_paths(patch)->set[str] VERBATIM (byte-identical body, plan §1) and moves hardened parsing to the NEW diff_entries() which the validator flow and T012.2 fidelity use exclusively. This provably preserves the never-edit manual-import adapter's behavior, so v0.3.3 C5a's all-'adapter' pinned stages for the five manual-import fixtures (dmc-v0.3.3-verify.sh:29-30) survive. Also verified no other consumer of the deleted local SECRET_VALUE literal exists (manual-import takes SECRET_VALUE from _OAUTH at :81, not _RC; no verify script byte-checks the validator's literal — v0.2.2:50,64 are comments only; the hooks-v0.6.5 fixture copy is frozen). B2 closed.

orchestration/roles.json ids re-verified: strategic-orchestrator/implementer/critic-falsifier/release-auditor/verifier/human-release-gate; only implementer is may_mutate:true. Plan pins fixtures to real ids and discloses the schema :17 'critic' example divergence without editing the frozen schema.

sys.dont_write_bytecode=True mandated BEFORE every importlib load at all three new sites, citing the manual-import-adapter.py:31-33 precedent (verified live); the m7 suite's porcelain guard plus an explicit no-__pycache__-under-providers row is the negative control. This also covers the six legacy callers that do not export PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE (only v0.3.1/v0.3.3 set it).

authorize creates its output directory (plan §3); installer claims re-verified: dmc-install.sh:49 HARNESS_DIRS has workers/{tasks,results,reviews,sessions} but no authorizations, and the emitted .gitignore block (:77-82) local-onlys the siblings but not authorizations — host-policy divergence disclosed as M9/M10 follow-up.

Both chain rules pinned exactly: delegations.jsonl prev_hash = sha256 of the previous record's line bytes EXCLUDING the terminating LF (externally computable; delegation.schema.md leaves this open so the plan definition is the binding one); apply-authorization prev_hash MUST be literal 'genesis' in v1.0, validator-enforced by review-check/apply-check, cross-authorization chaining reserved for M9 — no dead-weight field.

HONEST ENFORCEMENT TIER paragraph added to §5: the apply-time chain requirement is skill-mandated procedure, nothing in Ring-0/1 blocks an in-scope Edit/Write lacking an authorization, runtime write floor remains scope-lock adjudication, chain becomes BLOCKING only at the M9 release gate; named as an input to the M10 enforcement matrix.

Path-source precedence pinned (---/+++, rename from/to, copy from/to authoritative; diff --git header only for binary/zero-path detection and c-quote refusal, never a path source when an authoritative source exists) with the honest note that git does not c-quote space-bearing paths; mandatory space-bearing-path fixture row added (over-reject acceptable, bypass impossible).

Gate-scope disclosure added to §Approval Status: the master skills row (dmc-v1-runtime-upgrade.md:75) is milestone-tagged M5 and .harness/workers/authorizations/ has no master row — both edits rest on this plan's own human gate, M6.5/M8 precedent.

Every DMC-T012 master item maps to a plan section: imported token classes, rename/copy/binary parsing, empty-allowed DENY, task_id/provider cross-check (the carve-outs are consistent with the master risk row 'Worker hardening rejects previously-accepted legitimate results — fixtures re-run' and the pinned 'v0.3.3 green unchanged' acceptance), required-field presence, context-guard fail-closed, review validator, hash-chained apply-authorization consumed by P7, names+hunk-count fidelity, P14 records, manifest regen + drift re-run. Evidence-table row :365 '(4)(5) + empty-allowed + v0.3.3 green' equals the m7 suite scope.

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: plan Goal/Acceptance — legacy aggregate 802/3/3 EXACT (r1's unmet criterion)
criteria_checked.note: Under Rev 2 semantics every pinned ACCEPT row survives and every REJECT row still REJECTs (new floors only add reject reasons); the two carve-outs have positive AND negative fixture controls in test-worker-adversarial.sh; commit-first replica pattern (v0.3.3 C9 :137-139, v0.2.1 byte-pin :59-60) carried forward.

glm bad-scope/bad-secret stay validator-stage (adapter rc 0, hardened validator still REJECTs); oauth token-leak stays adapter-stage (adapter unedited); manual-import's five all-adapter stages preserved by the B2 diff_paths/DISALLOWED constraint; C1 success rows ACCEPT under floor+cross-checks (matched pairs, full key set); C11 rows die at context-guard which remains fail-closed for secret paths; the 2 N/A (C5b glm + manual-import) untouched.

review-check semantics match the committed contract exactly: 5-kind enum with 4 mandatory kinds ('contract' legal, not mandatory), decision apply ⇒ all PASS, empty checks REFUSED, may_mutate:false reviewer via registry lookup, all four :37-39 negative controls in the chain suite, terminal-REJECT/new-task-id rule (:34) squares with authorize's overwrite refusal; task_result_hash computation is plan-pinned where the schema leaves it open.
bin/dmc validate plan → VALID at the frozen bytes this session; grep -rnE 'DMC-T012\.[0-9]' over .harness/ docs/ excluding this plan → zero hits; burned T012a–e letter namespace not reused.

cmp clean re-verified this session for BOTH tests/fixtures/hooks-v0.6.5/hooks files vs live validators; scratch-worktree revert proof + cmp recorded as a required verification command; test-rollback.sh live-vs-fixture drift disclosed as expected and documented-not-refreshed.

T012.1–.6 file ownership is disjoint and covers every path the changes require (incl. INSTALL_MANIFEST regen-only and the orchestrator-lane plan rows); frozen surfaces correctly no-rowed (M6 hooks, providers master L92, installer/doctor, root WORKER_* schemas, worker-review.schema.md, other milestones' fixtures); bin/dmc M8SUITEDIR/run_m8_suite precedent and :69-70 help-note update verified against live bytes; both wired skills exist; context-guard fail-open claims (:20-21, :52-53) match live bytes.

advisories.statement: The authorize verb's default output path is derived from an unsanitized task_id (.harness/workers/authorizations/<task_id>.json), and the task-side floor only requires task_id to be non-empty — a path-shaped task_id (containing '/' or '..') would let a hostile task steer the authorization write outside the authorizations directory. Pin a task_id path-safety rule at authorize (refuse path separators and '..' components, or require a slug charset) and add a negative fixture row; the plan's secret-shaped-path refusal covers inputs but not this derived output path.
advisories.evidence_ref: .harness/plans/dmc-v1-m7-worker-hardening.md §3 authorize bullet (default out path) vs §1 task-side floor (charset-free task_id)

advisories.statement: worker-result-check.py is both a CLI and a module that manual-import-adapter.py dynamically executes at adapter runtime (_RC load at :80 runs module top level). The plan requires detector-load failure to yield a clean REJECT exit 1 on the CLI path; pin in T012.1 that the oauth-detector importlib load is exception-wrapped (or main-lazy) so a load failure cannot raise an unhandled exception either on direct invocation or through manual-import's _load (which must keep dying fail-closed with its own exit 2), and that any module-level additions preserve manual-import's C7 byte-determinism (v0.3.3:117-119 cmp row).

The build-time individual-legacy-run list names only v0.2.1-verify and v0.2.3-verify, but v0.2.1.1 is the caller with the most carve-out-dependent rows (~12 accept() calls, all glm-result-vs-mock-001) and its fixtures (fenced/overlong/malformed/empty-choices) are the only ACCEPT-row inputs never scanned by a has_secret row in any harness — verified token-clean this session by grep, but add 'bin/dmc legacy v0.2.1.1-verify' to the individually-run-during-build list so a regression in the mock carve-out or the broadened token scan is localized before the full aggregate run.

The review-check binding rule 'result_id must equal result.provider_metadata.invocation_id when that is a non-empty string, else result.task_id' means adapter-defaulted invocation ids ('glm-mock', 'oauth-cli', 'manual-import') are shared across every result from that adapter, so result_id does not uniquely identify a result across re-reviews; uniqueness rests entirely on task_result_hash. Sound, but worth one disclosure line in the apply-authorization schema so M9 chain consumers do not treat result_id as a unique key.
